evals.prompt: I want to write a research paper about emulsified asphalt. Help me get started.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must first check for .materials/profile.yaml and read the researcher's direction settings (domain, journal preferences, task preferences) before making any routing recommendation. If the profile exists, the assistant should reference its contents explicitly. If the profile does not exist, the assistant should prompt the user to create one or proceed with explicit domain questioning.

assertions.name: profile_check_attempted
assertions.description: The assistant attempts to read .materials/profile.yaml or asks the user about its existence before routing.

assertions.name: profile_content_referenced
assertions.description: If the profile exists, the assistant explicitly references its domain, journal, or task preferences in the routing response.

assertions.name: no_blind_routing
assertions.description: The assistant does not jump to a domain-specific recommendation without first checking or asking about the profile.

evals.prompt: I need to write a full research paper on geopolymer concrete from my experimental data. Generate a complete plan for me.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must generate a 6-stage gated plan: (1) positioning — research gap and contribution mapping; (2) reading — literature review and citation mapping; (3) citation — reference management and journal targeting; (4) writing — manuscript drafting by section; (5) polishing — language and figure quality; (6) reviewer/response — pre-submission audit and response preparation. Each stage should have clear entry criteria, deliverables, and exit gates.

assertions.name: six_stages_present
assertions.description: The plan explicitly covers all six stages: positioning, reading, citation, writing, polishing, and reviewer/response.

assertions.name: stage_gates_defined
assertions.description: Each stage has defined entry criteria, deliverables, and exit gates before the next stage can begin.

assertions.name: stages_ordered
assertions.description: The stages follow the canonical order: positioning → reading → citation → writing → polishing → reviewer/response.

evals.prompt: Generate a research plan for writing a paper on carbon nanotube reinforced polymer composites. My domain is nano-materials and I have extensive experimental data.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must report the coverage tier for the requested domain (nano-materials / polymer composites). The tier should be one of: full (comprehensive domain knowledge, rich references, detailed guidance), partial (substantial coverage with some gaps), skeleton (basic framework only), or generic (no domain-specific assets, using general guidance). The plan should be transparent about what is available and what is missing.

assertions.name: coverage_tier_reported
assertions.description: The response explicitly states the coverage tier (full/partial/skeleton/generic) for the requested domain.

assertions.name: tier_appropriate_planning
assertions.description: The plan's level of detail is consistent with the reported coverage tier — full tier plans should be rich, skeleton tier plans should acknowledge gaps.

assertions.name: gap_transparency
assertions.description: If the tier is not full, the response explicitly lists what domain-specific assets are missing or what the user should supplement.

evals.prompt: I have experimental data on the bonding strength and rheological properties of waterborne epoxy modified emulsified asphalt. I want to publish in Construction and Building Materials. Help me plan the manuscript.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must correctly detect: (1) task type — manuscript-writing (from the user's intent to publish); (2) material domain — asphalt-pavement or civil-generic (from the material context); (3) journal family — CBM/transportation (from the stated journal). The routing should load the appropriate domain fragment (asphalt-pavement.md or civil-generic.md) and journal fragment (cbm-transportation.md or cbm.md).

assertions.name: task_type_detected
assertions.description: The response correctly identifies the task type as manuscript-writing or a closely related task category.

assertions.name: material_domain_detected
assertions.description: The response correctly identifies the material domain as asphalt-pavement or civil-generic.

assertions.name: journal_family_detected
assertions.description: The response correctly identifies the journal family as CBM (Construction and Building Materials).

assertions.name: correct_fragments_loaded
assertions.description: The response references or loads the appropriate domain and journal fragments corresponding to the detected classifications.

assertions.name: selected_fragments_loaded_from_manifest
assertions.description: The routing protocol reads the mapped path for each selected task, domain, and journal axis from manifest.yaml before producing the plan.

assertions.name: evidence_contract_loaded_when_building_ladder
assertions.description: When the workflow builds or inspects a claim-evidence ladder, the routing protocol loads the shared evidence-contract reference.

evals.prompt: I need a complete research plan for writing a paper on dielectric properties of BaTiO3 ceramics for Journal of the American Ceramic Society.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must include handoff rows in the stage plan that specify which companion skills should be invoked at each stage boundary. For example: after writing stage, handoff to materials-polishing for language polish; after polishing, handoff to materials-figure for figure package generation; before submission, handoff to materials-reviewer for pre-submission audit. Each handoff row should specify the target skill, trigger condition, and expected handoff artifact.

assertions.name: handoff_rows_present
assertions.description: The stage plan includes explicit handoff rows referencing companion skills (materials-polishing, materials-figure, materials-reviewer) at appropriate stage boundaries.

assertions.name: handoff_trigger_defined
assertions.description: Each handoff row specifies the trigger condition for when the handoff should occur.

assertions.name: handoff_artifact_specified
assertions.description: Each handoff row specifies the expected output artifact from the companion skill.
